target.name: Roam Robotics
target.founder: Tim Swift, Mallory Ulaszek, Saul Griffith

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Roam Robotics is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $16.4M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable, with peer valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B across Series B staging.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a valuation proxy, applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implying a $33M valuation. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M)—yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $28M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $554M. The wide dispersion reflects substantial heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the outsized valuations of 1X Technologies. Key uncertainties include stage-adjusted comparability—most peers are Series B—and whether Roam's specific technology, market positioning, and commercialization progress justify positioning toward the lower or upper end of the peer range. Revenue, burn rate, and customer traction data would refine this estimate materially.
